scope: Kernel-local resolver status vocabulary for KROAD-005. This file defines status semantics only; it is not a Resolver MVP and does not resolve real decisions.

allowed_resolver_statuses.meaning: A future resolver rule may select exactly one allowed option only when the rule and required evidence are satisfied.
allowed_resolver_statuses.evidence_policy: required evidence tier and required evidence refs must be satisfied before the status can be emitted
allowed_resolver_statuses.finality_policy: not production_ready and not downstream_validated unless separate downstream/runtime evidence exists

allowed_resolver_statuses.meaning: A future resolver rule may leave more than one allowed option valid and require bounded Architect or downstream choice inside the allowed set.
allowed_resolver_statuses.selected_option_policy: allowed_option_set_required; final selected option may remain provisional
allowed_resolver_statuses.evidence_policy: explicit missing evidence and limitations must be recorded

allowed_resolver_statuses.meaning: The rule or evidence is insufficient; the pipeline must halt, ask for repair, or return insufficient evidence instead of guessing.
allowed_resolver_statuses.selected_option_policy: no final selected option; decision records should use option_id none

next_allowed_step: KROAD-006 may implement a small Resolver MVP for high-risk P0 families after this contract is merged and validated.
